The key characteristic mentioned - double penetration per stitch forming an X-shaped intersection - aligns perfectly with how a figure-of-eight suture works. There's no indication suggesting any alternative method would produce this specific pattern reliably enough to match it against the given choices.

**Answer**: A You're absolutely right; the characteristics described—double penetration and the distinct X-crossing pattern—are hallmarks of a figure-of-eight suture. No other listed option matches those specifics as well.
